name: Vidura
description: Vidura, also known as Dharmaja, was a wise and respected counselor in the court of the Kuru dynasty in the epic Mahabharata. Born from a maidservant and the sage Vyasa, Vidura was considered an incarnation of Yama, the god of death and justice. Despite his birth, he was known for his unparalleled wisdom, unwavering integrity, and profound understanding of dharma. He served as the prime minister to both Dhritarashtra and Yudhishthira, offering invaluable advice during times of crisis. Vidura's counsel was often ignored due to the political machinations of Duryodhana and Shakuni, leading to the devastating Kurukshetra War. He consistently championed righteousness and opposed injustice, even when it meant standing against his own family. He foresaw the impending doom of the Kuru dynasty due to their अधर्म (adharma – unrighteousness) and tried to prevent it. His insights into statecraft, ethics, and morality are compiled in the Vidura Neeti, a collection of his wise sayings. Vidura remained steadfast in his devotion to dharma, even in the face of immense suffering, making him a symbol of integrity and wisdom. He ultimately renounced his position and worldly attachments, choosing a life of asceticism and spiritual pursuit.
subtitle: The embodiment of wisdom and dharma.
achievements: Foresight and understanding of the future.
Remaining composed amidst chaos and injustice.

keyMyths.title: Birth of Vidura
keyMyths.description: Vidura was born from a maidservant and the sage Vyasa due to Ambika's (one of the queens) fear and closing her eyes during her encounter with Vyasa. He was an incarnation of Yama, the god of death and justice, cursed to be born as a mortal.

keyMyths.title: Vidura's Counsel
keyMyths.description: Vidura consistently offered wise counsel to Dhritarashtra and Yudhishthira, advising them on matters of statecraft, ethics, and morality. His advice was often ignored, leading to disastrous consequences for the Kuru dynasty.

keyMyths.title: Vidura Neeti
keyMyths.description: The Vidura Neeti is a collection of Vidura's wise sayings and teachings on various aspects of life, including politics, ethics, and personal conduct. It is considered a valuable source of wisdom and guidance.

sources.author: Vyasa
sources.description: The primary source for the life and teachings of Vidura.
sources.text: The primary source for the life and teachings of Vidura.

sources.author: C. Rajagopalachari
sources.description: A simplified retelling of the Mahabharata, including the story of Vidura.
